coach_note: Tonsillitis confirmed 24 Mar. First run back today (26 Mar) — 5km easy, HR 137 avg, felt OK. Throat still scratchy but fever-free for 3 days. Wellness markers show resting HR up slightly (43 vs usual 41-42) and HRV depressed (64 vs 84 pre-illness). Easing back in with easy running Thu-Sun this week. Plan adjusted: 4×2k on Mon 30 Mar (not 5×) to account for the illness week, progressing to 5×2k on Wed 8 Apr. Turbo sessions added weekly — sweet spot and VO2 intervals on the bike to maintain aerobic stimulus without impact stress. CTL 35.5, will rebuild quickly once volume returns. Six weeks of specific work remain before Bristol — still enough.

predictions.rationale: Paddock Wood 1:17:34 on a hilly course as a practice race. One week lost to tonsillitis absorbed by deep fitness base. First run back today showed normal HR response. 4×2k on Mon 30th eases back into quality work before progressing to 5×2k the following week. Six weeks of specific work remain — enough for the key race-pace sessions and tempos. Bristol is flat and fast. Wider prediction range reflects illness uncertainty but target remains realistic.

PB 16:11 set during marathon training with no 5K-specific work. HM build strengthening the aerobic engine. Illness has no material impact on this longer-term goal. Summer 5K block with specific speedwork on top of this base should deliver sub-16.
